>SpaceX literally loses money and its claim to future profit is Grok, a cash-burning AI model that is losing to GPT and Claude
>Twitter is breakeven at best
What's going on here? Are white boomers just throwing money at him because they don't like seeing Indians, Chinese and Jews running shit in tech? This is the only explanation for the Elon Musk phenomenon I can make any sense of.

62361459
>>62361452

but you have to start somewhere, and he certainly does stuff other companies can't.

item.title: How do I handle being lambasted and scrutinized when I finally publicize my business endeavors? — 15 replies — 60.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
How do I handle being lambasted and scrutinized when I finally publicize my business endeavors?

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-06-13 06:08 UTC

OP

How do I handle being lambasted and scrutinized when I finally publicize my business endeavors? Every time I start a new project it just feels like the mouth of the public (a poorly opinionated beast) opens up and tries to swallow me. A lot of their criticism is subjective as hell, so I couldn't give two shits about a lot of it. Some of it is helpful but it's tiring discerning between good advice and faggotry persuasion. The funny part is, despite the criticisms, my projects pretty much always succeed. I usually end my projects before they officially get anywhere because I immediately get scared of the publicity I start receiving. They are usually creative projects, but in some way they always have the capacity to make money. I start getting full blown panic attacks because of the amount of people shit talking me and threatening me. There's a lot of positivity given to me as well, but usually outweighed by the negativity. I am also the kind of person that doesn't necessarily want to make something everyone is going to like (at first) and I go against the grain, so it's a given that I will get haters. IDK maybe this is why people have social media managers and stuff like that... I just need a way to manage being public without it ruining my life and freaking me out. I hope that this makes sense.

Early replies (first 3)

62362161
>>62362152
>I hope that this makes sense.
It does not. Good day to you. I wish you well.

62362177
>>62362161
Let me put it simply; when my projects succeed I get fans whom harass me and it gives me panic attacks. How do I cope? How do i deal with being a public figure?

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-06-13 05:14 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62362011
>>62361985
In what time frame? In certain ways Jack Welch is an excellent CEO and in others he is one of the worst.

62362026
>>62361985
Richard Fuld

62362048
>>62361985
There have been a lot of dumb guys. First to come to mind is Apple's first CEO, Michael Scott. He was basically just some guy who worked in a chip factory so they made him the CEO for that tiny bit of experience. After a little bit of success, he got stressed out and had a mental breakdown. He fired most of the company while laughing. Then afterwards he got really drunk in the office to celebrate everyone who still had a job.

As of right now, Ubisoft's CEO, Yves Guillemot, is a total moron. The gaming industry has changed a lot, yet he refuses to innovate. He won't even sell the company unless he gets to remain as CEO, which scared off potential investors. Rather than making new games, his plan is to remake old games. The problem with remaking old games is that his company has essentially been doing that for the past 15 years, which is the entire reason why sales are down in the first place. We are seeing Ubisoft collapse in real time.

(srs post) thoughts on my nostradamus'ing? Good? Meh? Dumb?

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-06-13 03:08 UTC

OP

June 11: chop as market gets ready for pump on spacex trading day

June 12 - June 16: iran FUD is metabolized and market goes up on spacex


June 17 - June 22: FOMC on June 16 EOD causes fud, plus more iran fears

June 22 - Oct 10: NDQ floats upwards 40% while S&P chops and eventually goes up 10%

Oct 10: NDQ reaches a generational top - OpenAI IPOs - Fed announces more hikes due to AI labor inflation - republicans lose congress making trump a lame duck president = no more stimulus grift to pump markets
Oct 10, 2026 - ~Oct 10 2027: as a consequence of the lame duck presidency and the lack of stimulus and the selling of AI venture capital bags onto retail, S&P sells off 40% to round trip all the way back down to 5000

~Oct 10 2027: anthropic ipos

~Oct 10 2027 onwards: pretty much a new 20 year cycle begins and most likely the end of humanity as we birth sentience of machines.

62360629
>>62348278
>They are getting cooked by real handhelds now and I’m going to be buying another handheld isn’t a switch for my son
Making money out of handheld consoles depends on just 2 factors: How cheap can you make them and how much money can you get from software sales on that console.
Nintendo are the only handheld manufacturer with enough sales volume to get the best manufacturing prices. They are able to even commision custom silicon like Apple does with their phones to optimize things further, something that is completely out of reach of smaller players.
Nintendo also gets a ton of money from game sales and subscriptions as they limit things like the switch internet unless you pay and own game franchises that only release on their hardware.
Meanwhile their competitors need to spend more money to make an equivalent product and the only one that actually get revenue from software sales is Valve.
There's always going to be more powerful handhelds than whatever Nintendo makes as they don't design their stuff for performance, but there's not going to be a single handheld product that is as effective at generating net income as Nintendo's until someone starts getting closer to their sales volume.
Whenever Nintendo doesn't offer the best performance per dollar ratio, it is because they are selling their hardware at a premium due to how strong their brand is, not because they are incapable of making money at a lower pricepoint. Their competitors meanwhile are always running razor thin margins and any major impact to manufacturing costs needs an immediate readjustment to the sales price, as exemplified by the huge price bump the Deck got this month.

TL;DR: Nintendo doesn't make shit deals out of their products because they are incapable of offering something better, but because they have such an advantageous position that they don't need to. If someone ever gets close enough to force them to compete on prices/performance more, they will.

item.title: Remember the classic Wall Street rule: "When the shoeshine boy gives you stock tips, it's time to sell."Fast forward to… — 25 replies — 16.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Remember the classic Wall Street rule: "When the shoeshine boy gives you stock tips, it's time to sell."Fast forward to…

Open thread • Replies: 25 • Updated: 2026-06-13 04:43 UTC

OP

Remember the classic Wall Street rule: "When the shoeshine boy gives you stock tips, it's time to sell."Fast forward to today, June 12, 2026.SpaceX goes public (ticker: SPCX) as the largest IPO in human history.The absolute pinnacle of "shoeshine boy" indicators unfolds before our eyes.

Look at the massive retail mania.Brokers are literally bending over backward to push high-risk equity onto everyday people.

Fidelity casually drops its early SpaceX asset requirement by 99.996%.Went from a $500,000 balance requirement down to a measly $2,000 just so anyone can buy in.

Everyday normies who have never read a balance sheet are getting allocations.SpaceX targets a massive 20% to 30% retail allocation just to feed the public frenzy.

Early replies (first 3)

62361322
Look at the underlying financial reality.The stock prices in pure unadulterated "Musk premium" while totally ignoring the numbers.

Priced at $135 a share, meaning investors paid 94 times trailing revenue.The company has an accumulated deficit of $41.3 billion.Lost $4.9 billion net last year alone.

The $2+ trillion valuation literally assumes a 75-fold earnings growth by 2035.

Valuation relies on unproven sci-fi assumptions like building AI data centers in outer space.

62361333
>>62361316
they're spending worthless dollars and they know it.

$135 per share? That's how much I make per employee per hour doing nothing. That's the value of an hour's work in dollars in the US

basically worthless dollars.

62361335
peak euphoria.Retail hype pumps the stock up 19% on day one to close at $160.95. Elon becomes the world's first trillionaire

SpaceX immediately starts selling merch reading "The future is public" at the listing. nasdaq rules mean it will be fast-tracked into the Nasdaq-100 in just 15 trading days.

Passive retirement funds and normie index accounts will be forced to buy it automatically.

item.title: Some 16 year old told me he had worked a full week nonstop, so he was told he had to take the next five days off. — 82 replies — 14.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Some 16 year old told me he had worked a full week nonstop, so he was told he had to take the next five days off.

Open thread • Replies: 82 • Updated: 2026-06-13 00:21 UTC

OP

Some 16 year old told me he had worked a full week nonstop, so he was told he had to take the next five days off. I told him that was good.

I told him that most people will not make it in today’s economy when $150,000 is considered middle class. He replied that $150,000 is a lot of money. I said sure, but the average American salary is around $65,000.

Then I asked him what kind of work he did, and he said he did some low level blue collar welding. I told him blue collar work is terrible. He responded that welders can make over $100,000 a year.

I told him that blue collar work is for low-IQ, uneducated people and that the adults in those jobs are losers and people who messed up their lives. I also said blue collar work is fun when you’re a kid, but the adults doing it are there because they’re losers.

It turned out that three blue collar workers had been listening to our conversation and told the kid not to listen to me because I didn’t know what I was talking about.

I then told the kid that when he grows up, he will realize people look down on blue collar workers for a reason and that blue collar adults are not the kind of people he should want to know.

The blue collar workers called me ignorant, yet, from my perspective, they seemed to have chips on their shoulders and came across as unintelligent.

Why are blue- collar workers like this? Are they aware of how the world perceives them?

I wanted to talk numbers because no way most blue collar makes over $100k. Can someone fill me in?

>>62342700

Ash Grove. In that pic I was troubleshooting a truck loading issue. I discovered that our loading system was haunting loading due to some shutdown condition for a split second and tgen deciding to be happy again, most likely fluctuations in the scale wait from the truck or scale shaking while loading. The momentary blip breaks a logic latch forcing a retard of the loading process each time. Now I get to find out how to use timer off delays in unity pro and proworx32 to filter out that momentary blip. Every day is a new problem I have to figure out and I enjoy that.
